metadata.name: Azure Security Center (Defender for Cloud)
metadata.description:
This module deploys an Azure Security Center (Defender for Cloud) Configuration.

For the associated policy assignments (e.g., Deploy-MDFC-Config), use the [ALZ Bicep Accelerator](https://aka.ms/alz/acc/bicep) or reference the [Deploy-MDFC-Config policy initiative](https://www.azadvertizer.net/azpolicyinitiativesadvertizer/Deploy-MDFC-Config_20240319.html) directly.

Required. The Defender plan name (e.g., VirtualMachines, SqlServers, AppServices, StorageAccounts, Containers, KeyVaults, Arm, CloudPosture, Api, CosmosDbs, OpenSourceRelationalDatabases, SqlServerVirtualMachines).
Required. The pricing tier for the Defender plan.
Optional. The sub-plan to select when more than one is available (e.g., P1 or P2 for VirtualMachines, DefenderForStorageV2 for StorageAccounts).
Optional. If set to True, prevents descendants from overriding this pricing configuration. Only available for subscription-level pricing.
Optional. List of extensions offered under the plan (e.g., OnUploadMalwareScanning, SensitiveDataDiscovery, AgentlessVmScanning, ContainerSensor).
The type for a Defender plan configuration.
Required. The name of the IoT Security Solution.
Required. The resource group to deploy the IoT Security Solution into.
Required. The workspace resource ID.
Required. The display name of the IoT Security Solution.
Optional. Status of the IoT Security Solution.
Optional. List of additional export to workspace data options.
Optional. Disabled data sources.
Required. IoT Hub resource IDs.
Optional. Properties of the IoT Security Solution's user defined resources.
Optional. List of the configuration status for each recommendation type.
The type for IoT Security Solution data.
Required. The name of the security automation resource.
Required. The resource group to deploy the automation into.
Optional. Location for the automation resource. Defaults to the deployment location.
Optional. Tags for the automation resource.
Optional. Description of the security automation.
Optional. Whether the security automation is enabled. Defaults to true.
Required. The scopes on which the automation logic is applied (subscription or resource group IDs).
Required. The source event types which evaluate the automation rules.
Required. The actions triggered when all rules evaluate to true.
The type for a security automation (continuous export) configuration.
Required. The selected vulnerability assessments provider (e.g., MdeTvm for Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management).
The type for server vulnerability assessments settings.
Required. The name (GUID) of the security standard.
Required. The display name of the security standard.
Optional. Description of the security standard.
Optional. List of assessment keys to apply to the standard scope.
Optional. List of cloud providers the standard supports.
Optional. The policy set definition ID associated with the standard.
The type for a custom security standard.
Required. The name (GUID) of the standard assignment.
Required. The resource ID of the assigned standard.
Optional. Description of the assignment.
Optional. Display name of the assignment.
Optional. The effect of the assignment (e.g., Audit, Exempt).
Optional. List of scopes excluded from the assignment.
Optional. Expiration date of the assignment.
Optional. Additional metadata for the assignment.
The type for a standard assignment.
Required. The name (GUID) of the custom recommendation.
Required. The display name of the recommendation.
Optional. Description of the recommendation.
Required. The severity of the recommendation.
Optional. The security issue category.
Required. KQL query representing the recommendation results.
Optional. The remediation description.
Optional. List of cloud providers the recommendation applies to.
The type for a custom security recommendation.
Required. The setting name (MCAS, WDATP, WDATP_EXCLUDE_LINUX_PUBLIC_PREVIEW, WDATP_UNIFIED_SOLUTION, or Sentinel).
Required. The kind of setting (DataExportSettings for MCAS/WDATP, AlertSyncSettings for Sentinel).
Required. Whether the setting is enabled.
The type for a Defender for Cloud integration setting (MCAS, WDATP, Sentinel).
Required. The name (GUID) of the assessment metadata.
Required. The display name of the assessment.
Optional. Description of the assessment.
Required. The severity of the assessment.
Required. The assessment type (BuiltIn, CustomPolicy, CustomerManaged, VerifiedPartner).
Optional. Categories the assessment belongs to.
Optional. The implementation effort required.
Optional. The remediation description.
Optional. Threats the assessment mitigates.
Optional. The user impact of the assessment.
The type for a custom assessment metadata definition.
Required. The unique name of the suppression rule.
Required. The alert type to suppress. Use '*' for all alert types.
Required. The reason for dismissing the alert.
Required. The state of the rule (Enabled, Disabled, or Expired).
Optional. Comment regarding the rule.
Optional. Expiration date of the rule in UTC. If not provided, the rule will not expire.
Optional. The suppression conditions — all conditions must be true to suppress the alert.
The type for an alert suppression rule.
Required. The unique name of the governance rule.
Required. The display name of the governance rule.
Required. The rule type (e.g., Integrated, ServiceNow).
Required. The source resource type (e.g., Assessments).
Required. The rule priority (0-1000, lower is higher priority).
Required. The owner source for the rule.
Required. The condition sets that determine when the rule applies.
Optional. Description of the governance rule.
Optional. Whether the rule is disabled.
Optional. Whether there is a grace period on the governance rule.
Optional. Remediation timeframe (e.g., 7.00:00:00 for 7 days).
Optional. Whether to include member scopes.
Optional. Excluded scopes to filter out descendants.
Optional. Email notification settings for the governance rule.
The type for a governance rule.
Optional. Location deployment metadata.
Optional. Microsoft Defender for Cloud pricing plan configurations. Each entry specifies a Defender plan name, pricing tier, and optional subPlan, enforce, and extensions settings.
Optional. Security contact configuration for Microsoft Defender for Cloud notifications.
Optional. Device Security group properties.
Optional. Security Solution data for IoT.

Optional. Continuous export (security automation) configurations. Each entry creates a Microsoft.Security/automations resource that exports alerts and/or recommendations to a Log Analytics workspace, Event Hub, or Logic App.
Optional. Server vulnerability assessment settings. Configures the vulnerability assessment provider (e.g., Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management).
Optional. Custom security standards to create. Each entry defines a standard with assessments to apply.
Optional. Standard assignments to create. Each entry assigns a security standard to a scope.
Optional. Custom security recommendations to create. Each entry defines a custom recommendation with a KQL query.
Optional. Defender for Cloud integration settings (MCAS, WDATP, Sentinel). Each entry enables or disables a specific integration.
Optional. Custom assessment metadata definitions. Used to define custom assessments that pair with custom recommendations.
Optional. Alert suppression rules to reduce alert noise. Each entry creates a suppression rule for a specific alert type.
Optional. Governance rules to drive security remediation. Each entry defines ownership, remediation timeframes, and notification settings.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.
Required. The name of the IoT Security Solution.
Optional. Location for the resource.
Optional. Tags for the resource.
Required. Security Solution properties.
The resource ID of the IoT Security Solution.
The name of the IoT Security Solution.
Required. The name of the security automation.
Optional. Location for the resource.
Optional. Tags for the resource.
Optional. Description of the security automation.
Optional. Whether the security automation is enabled.
Required. The scopes on which the automation logic is applied.
Required. The source event types which evaluate the rules.
Required. The actions triggered when rules evaluate to true.
The resource ID of the security automation.
The name of the security automation.
The name of the security center.
The list of Defender plan names that were configured.
